landing.heroSubtitle: Production-ready template with Expo, Tamagui, auth, i18n, analytics, and Fastify backend. One codebase — deploy to App Store, Google Play, and Web.

landing.featureCrossPlatformDesc: Write once, deploy to App Store, Google Play & Web. Powered by Expo and React Native — one codebase for all platforms.
landing.featureThemingDesc: Beautiful design system with Tamagui. System-aware theme switching out of the box.
landing.featureI18nDesc: Internationalization ready with English, Russian, Spanish, and Japanese.
landing.featureAuthDesc: JWT auth with email/password, Google OAuth, and SMS OTP. Role-based access control included.
landing.featureBackendDesc: Fastify API with Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Nodemailer for email via any SMTP.
landing.featureAnalyticsDesc: PostHog integration with session tracking, screen analytics, and admin dashboard.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.selectPage: Select a page from the sidebar to get started
docs.contentQuickStart:
## Quick Start

### Full Setup (recommended)

```
pwsh scripts/setup.ps1
```

Installs dependencies, starts Docker (PostgreSQL + Redis), generates `.env` with JWT secrets, pushes DB schema.

```
pwsh scripts/setup.ps1 -AdminEmail your@email.com
```

Then start:

```
npm run dev
```

### Frontend Only

```
npm install && npm run dev:mobile
```

Backend must be running for authentication to work.
docs.contentPrerequisites:
## Prerequisites

- **Node.js** 18+ (recommended: 20 LTS)
- **npm** 9+
- **Docker** — for PostgreSQL and Redis
- **PowerShell** (pwsh) — for setup scripts
- **Expo Go** or dev build — for mobile testing

### Optional

- **EAS CLI** — for production builds (`npm install -g eas-cli`)
- **Xcode** — for iOS simulator
- **Android Studio** — for Android emulator
docs.contentProjectStructure:
## Project Structure

```
MVPTemplate/
├── apps/
│   ├── mobile/          # Expo (iOS/Android/Web)
│   │   ├── app/         # File-based routes
│   │   └── src/         # Features, hooks, services
│   ├── backend/         # Fastify API
│   │   ├── src/modules/ # auth, users, admin, push, payments, analytics, email
│   │   ├── src/database/ # Drizzle schema
│   │   └── docker/      # Docker configs
│   └── extension/       # Chrome extension (optional)
│       └── src/         # React + Vite + CRXJS
├── packages/
│   ├── ui/              # Tamagui components, landing page
│   ├── auth/            # Auth forms, providers, flows
│   ├── payments/        # Stripe, PayPal, Polar, YooKassa
│   ├── onboarding/      # Wizard + CoachMark tour
│   ├── store/           # Zustand stores
│   ├── i18n/            # 4 locales (EN/RU/ES/JA)
│   ├── docs/            # In-app documentation engine
│   ├── template-config/ # Feature flags, color schemes
│   ├── lib/             # MMKV, secure storage
│   ├── config/          # Shared configuration
│   └── analytics/       # PostHog
└── scripts/             # PowerShell utilities
```
docs.contentEnvVars:
## Environment Variables

### Backend (`apps/backend/.env`)

Auto-generated by `setup.ps1`, or configure manually:

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | — | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `REDIS_URL` | — | Redis connection string |
| `JWT_ACCESS_SECRET` | — | 32+ char secret for access tokens |
| `JWT_REFRESH_SECRET` | — | 32+ char secret for refresh tokens |
| `PORT` | `3000` | Server port |
| `CORS_ORIGIN` | `http://localhost:8081` | Frontend URL |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | — | Enables Google sign-in |

### Mobile (`apps/mobile/.env`)

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend URL (default: `http://localhost:3000`) |
| `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID` | Enables Google sign-in button |
docs.contentDatabaseSetup:
## Database Setup

The template uses **PostgreSQL 16** with **Drizzle ORM**.

### Start Database

```
pwsh scripts/docker-start.ps1
```

Or manually:

```
docker compose -f apps/backend/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

### Schema Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run db:push -w apps/backend` | Push schema to DB |
| `npm run db:studio -w apps/backend` | Open Drizzle Studio GUI |
| `npm run db:generate -w apps/backend` | Generate migrations |
| `npm run db:migrate -w apps/backend` | Run migrations |

### Reset Database

```
pwsh scripts/reset-db.ps1
```

Drops all tables and re-pushes the schema.
docs.contentAuth:
## Authentication

JWT-based auth with access + refresh token rotation.

- **Access token**: 15 min expiry, sent in `Authorization` header
- **Refresh token**: 30 day expiry, stored securely on device
- **Rate limiting**: 30 req/min on auth endpoints (Redis-backed)

### Google Sign-In (optional)

1. Create OAuth client in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)
2. Set `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID` in mobile `.env`
3. Set `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` in backend `.env`

The Google button only appears when the client ID is configured.

### Email Verification (optional)

Set `EMAIL_ENABLED=true` in backend `.env` to enable:
- Verification email on registration
- Password reset flow
- Welcome email after verification
docs.contentSms:
## SMS Verification

Phone number verification via 6-digit SMS OTP. Disabled by default — activates when `SMS_PROVIDER` is set.

### Supported Providers

- **Twilio** — international coverage
- **SMSC.ru** — Russia/CIS

### Setup

**Twilio:**
```
SMS_PROVIDER=twilio
TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=ACxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=your-auth-token
TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER=+1234567890
```

**SMSC.ru:**
```
SMS_PROVIDER=smsc
SMSC_LOGIN=your-login
SMSC_PASSWORD=your-password
SMSC_SENDER=YourApp
```

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/auth/send-phone-code` | — | Send 6-digit OTP to phone |
| `POST` | `/auth/verify-phone` | — | Verify phone with OTP |

### Admin Settings

Admin Panel → API Settings → SMS:
- Toggle **Require phone verification** to block sign-in for unverified numbers
- Switch the active provider (Twilio ↔ SMSC.ru) without restarting the server

### Database

OTP codes are stored in `phone_verification_codes` table with a 10-minute expiry. Codes are invalidated after successful verification.
docs.contentTheming:
## Theming & Dark Mode

The template uses **Tamagui v2** for theming with light and dark modes.

### Theme Configuration

Colors are defined in `packages/ui/tamagui.config.ts`.

### Theme Switching

Users can cycle between: **System** → **Light** → **Dark**

Theme preference is persisted in MMKV storage.

### Using Theme in Components

```tsx
import { useTheme } from 'tamagui'

function MyComponent() {
  const theme = useTheme()
  return <View style={{ backgroundColor: theme.background.val }} />
}
```

### Theme Tokens

| Token | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `$background` | Main background |
| `$color` | Primary text |
| `$accent` | Brand accent color |
| `$mutedText` | Secondary text |
| `$borderColor` | Borders and dividers |
| `$cardBackground` | Card surfaces |
docs.contentI18n:
## Internationalization

The template supports **4 languages**: English, Russian, Spanish, Japanese.

### Adding a New Language

1. Create `packages/i18n/src/locales/xx.json` (copy from `en.json`)
2. Translate all keys
3. Register in `packages/i18n/src/index.ts`:
   - Add to `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES` array
   - Add to `LANGUAGE_LABELS` map
   - Import and add to `resources`

### Using Translations

```tsx
import { useTranslation } from '@mvp/i18n'

function MyComponent() {
  const { t } = useTranslation()
  return <Text>{t('common.loading')}</Text>
}
```

Language auto-detects from device settings and can be manually switched in Settings.
docs.contentPushNotifications:
## Push Notifications

Built with **expo-notifications** + **expo-server-sdk**.

### Setup

1. Create an account at [expo.dev](https://expo.dev)
2. Go to **Settings** → **Access tokens** → **Create Token**
3. Set `EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN` in `apps/backend/.env`
4. Restart the backend — the feature flag activates automatically

### Architecture

- **Device registration**: on login, the app requests push permissions and sends the Expo push token to `POST /api/push/register`
- **Sending**: admin panel → Notify tab → enter title/body → Send to All
- **Backend flow**: creates notification records → sends push via Expo → emits SSE events
- **Receiving**: notifications appear in system tray (native) and in-app Notification Center

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/push/register` | Yes | Register device push token |
| `DELETE` | `/push/unregister` | Yes | Remove device push tokens |
| `POST` | `/push/send` | Admin | Send notification to users |
| `GET` | `/push/history` | Admin | Notification send history |

### User Settings

Users can toggle push notifications on/off in **Settings → Notifications**. Disabling removes device tokens from the server.

### Limitations

- Requires a **physical device** (not simulator)
- Use a **dev build** for full functionality (Expo Go has limited support)
- SSE works only on web; native uses push notifications
- `EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN` is optional for dev but recommended for production
docs.contentEmail:
## Email System

Email is disabled by default (`EMAIL_ENABLED=false`).

### Enable Email

1. Set `EMAIL_ENABLED=true` in backend `.env`
2. Configure SMTP:

**Gmail:**
```
SMTP_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
SMTP_USER=your@gmail.com
SMTP_PASS=your-app-password
```

**Development (no SMTP):**
Emails are captured by Ethereal — check server console for preview URLs.

### Features

- Email verification on registration
- Password reset flow
- Welcome email after verification
- Templates in 4 languages
docs.contentPayments:
## Payments

Payments are disabled by default (`PAYMENTS_ENABLED=false`). Supports two providers: **Stripe** (international) and **YooKassa** (Russia).

### Setup

1. Set `PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true` in `apps/backend/.env`
2. Configure at least one provider:

**Stripe:**
```
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_xxx
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_xxx
```

**YooKassa:**
```
YOOKASSA_SHOP_ID=your-shop-id
YOOKASSA_SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key
```

3. Run `npm run db:push -w apps/backend` to create tables
4. Seed default plans: `npm run db:seed -w apps/backend`
5. Restart the backend
6. Update `providerPriceId` for each plan via admin panel after creating products in your provider dashboard

### Architecture

- **Provider abstraction**: common `PaymentProvider` interface normalizes Stripe and YooKassa
- **Checkout flow**: user selects plan → redirect to hosted payment page → webhook confirms payment
- **Subscriptions**: Stripe Billing (native) or local DB management (YooKassa)

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/payments/plans` | — | List active plans |
| `POST` | `/payments/checkout` | Yes | Create checkout session |
| `GET` | `/payments/subscription` | Yes | Current subscription |
| `POST` | `/payments/cancel` | Yes | Cancel subscription |
| `GET` | `/payments/history` | Yes | Payment history |
| `GET` | `/payments/admin/stats` | Admin | Revenue stats |
| `POST` | `/payments/admin/plans` | Admin | Create a plan |

### Frontend

- **Pricing page** at `/pricing` — lists plans with subscribe buttons
- **Settings** → Manage Plan — view/cancel subscription
- **Admin panel** → Payments tab — revenue stats, active subscriptions, recent payments

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `PAYMENTS_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable/disable payments |
| `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` | — | Stripe secret key |
| `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | — | Stripe webhook secret |
| `YOOKASSA_SHOP_ID` | — | YooKassa shop ID |
| `YOOKASSA_SECRET_KEY` | — | YooKassa secret key |
docs.contentExtension:
## Chrome Extension

Optional browser extension template at `apps/extension/` with authentication, subscriptions, theming, and onboarding.

### Build & Load

```
npm run build:extension
```

Then: Chrome → `chrome://extensions` → Enable Developer mode → Load unpacked → select `apps/extension/dist/`

### Development

```
npm run dev:extension
```

### Modes

- **Sidebar** (default): opens as a Chrome side panel
- **Popup**: edit `src/manifest.ts` — set `default_popup` and remove `side_panel`

### Structure

- `src/manifest.ts` — Chrome Manifest V3
- `src/background.ts` — Service worker (token management, message routing)
- `src/components/` — AuthScreen, OnboardingScreen, MainScreen, SettingsTab, SubscriptionCard
- `src/services/api.ts` — JWT-authenticated API client with auto-refresh
- `src/hooks/` — useAuth, useTheme, useSubscription
- `src/styles/globals.css` — Tailwind CSS with light/dark theme variables

### Features

- JWT authentication (login/register) with auto token refresh
- 3-step onboarding wizard on first launch
- Subscription status display with upgrade link
- Light/dark/system theme switching via CSS variables
- Shared backend — same API as the mobile app
docs.contentOnboarding:
## Onboarding & Tours

The onboarding system runs automatically on first launch and can be replayed from Settings.

### Components

- **OnboardingWizard** — full-screen welcome modal with animated slides. Shown once; resets if the user clears app data
- **CoachMark tour** — interactive spotlight that highlights key UI elements one by one after the wizard completes

### Feature Flag

The entire onboarding system is controlled by a single flag:

```
EXPO_PUBLIC_ONBOARDING_ENABLED=true
```

Set to `false` to remove the wizard, coach marks, and the Settings → App Tour button entirely.

### Customizing Steps

Edit `apps/mobile/src/onboarding/steps.ts`:

```ts
export const WIZARD_STEPS: WizardStep[] = [
  { id: 'welcome', icon: 'sparkles-outline', title: '...', description: '...' },
  // add or remove steps here
]

export const HOME_COACH_STEPS: CoachMarkStep[] = [
  { id: 'home-stats', title: '...', description: '...' },
  // wrap target elements with <CoachMark stepId="home-stats"> in screen files
]
```

### Wrapping Elements with CoachMark

```tsx
import { CoachMark } from '@mvp/onboarding'

<CoachMark stepId="home-stats" scrollRef={scrollRef}>
  <MyComponent />
</CoachMark>
```

The `scrollRef` prop ensures the element scrolls into view before the spotlight appears.

### Package

All onboarding code lives in `packages/onboarding/` (`@mvp/onboarding`). Web uses `position: fixed` overlay instead of `Modal` to keep fonts working correctly.
docs.contentBuildProduction:
## Building for Production

### Mobile (EAS Build)

```
eas build --platform ios
eas build --platform android
```

Configure in `apps/mobile/eas.json`.

### Backend (Docker)

```
cd apps/backend
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t mvp-backend .
docker run -p 3000:3000 --env-file .env mvp-backend
```

### Web Export

```
npx expo export --platform web
```

### CI/CD

GitHub Actions workflows are included for:
- Lint + typecheck on every PR
- Docker build and deploy
- EAS Build triggers
docs.contentDocker:
## Docker Deployment

### Development

Start PostgreSQL + Redis:

```
pwsh scripts/docker-start.ps1
```

Or:

```
docker compose -f apps/backend/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

### Production

Build and run the backend:

```
cd apps/backend
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile -t mvp-backend .
docker run -p 3000:3000 --env-file .env mvp-backend
```

### Docker Compose (Full Stack)

The `docker-compose.yml` includes:
- **PostgreSQL 16** on port 5432
- **Redis 7** on port 6379

Data is persisted in Docker volumes.
docs.contentAddScreens:
## Adding New Screens

### Mobile Screen

1. Create `apps/mobile/app/my-screen.tsx`
2. Export a default component
3. Navigate with `router.push('/my-screen')`

### Tab Screen

1. Create `apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/my-tab.tsx`
2. Add `Tabs.Screen` in `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`
3. Add sidebar item in `_layout.tsx` WebRootLayout

### Backend Module

1. Create `apps/backend/src/modules/my-module/`
2. Add schema in `src/database/schema/`
3. Create routes, service, repository
4. Register in `src/app.ts`
5. Run `npm run db:push -w apps/backend`
docs.contentStyling:
## Styling Guide

### Tamagui Basics

All components use Tamagui's styled system:

```tsx
<YStack padding="$4" gap="$3" backgroundColor="$background">
  <Text color="$color" fontSize="$4">Hello</Text>
</YStack>
```

### Theme Tokens

Defined in `packages/ui/tamagui.config.ts`.

| Prefix | Example | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `$` + number | `$4` | Spacing/sizing scale |
| `$` + name | `$background` | Theme color tokens |
| `$` + radius | `$3` | Border radius scale |

### Custom Components

Add to `packages/ui/src/components/`:

1. Create `MyComponent.tsx`
2. Export from `components/index.ts`
3. Available as `import { MyComponent } from '@mvp/ui'`

### Animations

Available wrappers:
- `FadeIn` — opacity fade on mount
- `SlideIn` — slide from direction
- `ScalePress` — press scale effect
- `AnimatedListItem` — staggered list items
